he will rule as god; jisral, a symbolical name of jacob; also (typically) of his posterity

EZK.47.23.definition: to exist, i.e., be or become, come to pass (always emphatic, and not a mere copula or auxiliary)

EZK.47.23.gloss: And it shall come to pass that in what tribe
EZK.47.23.definition: a scion, i.e., (literally) a stick (for punishing, writing, fighting, ruling, walking, etc.) or (figuratively) a clan
who, which, what, that; also (as an adverb and a conjunction) when, where, how, because, in order that, etc
properly, to turn aside from the road (for a lodging or any other purpose), i.e., sojourn (as a guest); also to shrink, fear (as in a strange place);
properly, a guest; by implication, a foreigner
properly, nearness (used only as a preposition or an adverb), near; hence, generally, with, by, at, among, etc
there (transferring to time) then; often thither, or thence
to give, used with greatest latitude of application (put, make, etc.)
properly, something inherited, i.e., (abstractly) occupancy, or (concretely) an heirloom; generally an estate, patrimony or portion
the lord (used as a proper name of god only)
(the) self-existent or eternal; jeho-vah, jewish national name of god
